label: Cassandra
description: Connect to a Cassandra distributed wide-column database cluster using contact points, keyspace, and credentials.

form.title: Connect Cassandra
form.subtitle: Provide your cluster contact points, keyspace, and credentials to connect Anton to Cassandra.

methods.label: Username & Password
methods.description: Connect directly by supplying your Cassandra contact points, keyspace, username, and password. The simplest and most common path for any Cassandra cluster.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Cassandra Credentials

Use this method for any Cassandra cluster that has native authentication enabled (`PasswordAuthenticator` or a compatible authenticator).

### Steps

1. Identify your **contact points** — one or more `host:port` pairs for nodes in your cluster. Port `9042` is the default CQL native transport port.
   - Example: `cass-node1.example.com:9042, cass-node2.example.com:9042`
   - For local dev: `127.0.0.1:9042`
   - For DataStax Astra or managed services, check your cluster's **Connect** tab for the endpoint.
2. Determine your **keyspace** — the logical namespace your tables live in. You can list keyspaces with `DESCRIBE KEYSPACES;` in `cqlsh`.
3. Locate or create a Cassandra user with the appropriate permissions:
   - `CREATE ROLE anton WITH PASSWORD = '...' AND LOGIN = true;`
   - `GRANT SELECT ON KEYSPACE your_keyspace TO anton;` (add `MODIFY` if writes are needed)
4. Enter the values below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Contact points are comma-separated; Anton will try each in order and use the first reachable node to bootstrap the driver's cluster topology discovery.
- If your cluster uses SSL/TLS, enable the **Use TLS** toggle below.
- For DataStax Astra, use the **Secure Connect Bundle** method instead — Astra does not expose raw CQL ports.
- Credentials are stored encrypted in Anton's local vault.

fields.name: contact_points
fields.label: Contact Points
fields.description: Comma-separated list of host:port pairs for one or more nodes in your cluster. Port 9042 is the default.

fields.name: keyspace
fields.label: Keyspace
fields.description: The Cassandra keyspace (namespace) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Cassandra role/user with login privileges. Default superuser is 'cassandra' — create a dedicated role for Anton in production.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Cassandra user above.

fields.name: use_tls
fields.label: Use TLS
fields.description: Enable if your cluster requires SSL/TLS connections on the CQL port.

fields.name: datacenter
fields.label: Local Datacenter
fields.description: The name of the local datacenter for the driver's load-balancing policy. Leave blank to use the default ('datacenter1'). Check your cluster with 'SELECT data_center FROM system.local;' in cqlsh.

methods.label: Secure Connect Bundle (DataStax Astra)
methods.description: For DataStax Astra clusters. Upload or paste the path to your Secure Connect Bundle ZIP, along with your Astra client credentials.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via DataStax Astra Secure Connect Bundle

Astra clusters don't expose raw CQL ports. Instead, DataStax provides a **Secure Connect Bundle** (a ZIP file) that encapsulates TLS certificates and endpoint metadata.

### Steps

1. Log in to [DataStax Astra](https://astra.datastax.com) and open your database.
2. Click **Connect** → **Drivers** → choose any CQL driver → download the **Secure Connect Bundle** ZIP for your region.
3. In the **Token Management** section of your Astra database, generate a new token with the **Database Administrator** or appropriate role. Note the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — the secret is shown only once.
4. Enter your **keyspace** name (visible on the database Overview page).
5. Paste the full file path to the downloaded ZIP into **Secure Connect Bundle Path** below, then fill in the Client ID and Client Secret.

### Notes

- The Secure Connect Bundle is region-specific. If you have a multi-region database, download the bundle for the region closest to where Anton runs.
- The Client Secret is shown only once at generation time. If lost, generate a new token.
- Your Astra username in the CQL driver context is the **Client ID**, not your Astra login email.

fields.name: secure_connect_bundle_path
fields.label: Secure Connect Bundle Path
fields.description: Full local file path to the Secure Connect Bundle ZIP downloaded from the Astra console.

fields.name: keyspace
fields.label: Keyspace
fields.description: The Astra keyspace (namespace) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The Client ID from your Astra application token. Used as the CQL username.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The Client Secret from your Astra application token. Shown only once at generation — store it safely.
